title: Erigeron daveauanus
taxonomy: Erigeron daveauanus is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae, commonly known as the daisy or sunflower family. This classification places it within the larger group of flowering plants known as angiosperms. Erigeron daveauanus is further classified within the genus Erigeron, which consists of several species of daisy-like plants. The genus Erigeron is within the tribe Astereae, a group of flowering plants that are characterized by the presence of daisy-like flowers. Erigeron daveauanus is native to Madagascar, where it grows in a variety of habitats.

intro: Erigeron daveauanus, commonly known as the Daveau's fleabane, is a species of flowering plant in the daisy family (Asteraceae). This plant has been traditionally used to support immune function and reduce inflammation, with potential health benefits including the alleviation of symptoms associated with respiratory issues, such as congestion and coughs. The bioactive constituents of Erigeron daveauanus include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and essential oils, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Daveau's fleabane is often prepared as a tea or tincture, and may be used to treat a variety of ailments, including respiratory infections and digestive issues.
